title: Sample preconditioning before scanning electron microscopy (SEM)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"3dp8m","text":"Bacterial samples need to be preconditioned before visualized by scanning electron microscope (SEM). This protocol describes preconditioning methods of bacteria before SEM.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Obtain a small quantity of bacteria to be tested from a culture.

Centrifuge the samples at 5000 rpm, 4 °C, 10 minutes . Resuspend the pellet using 1X phosphate buffed saline (PBS). Repeat this step for three times. Keep the supernatant after the third centrifuge and preserve the samples at 4 °C .
Wash silica wafers with ddH 2 O and acetone for three times.
Add samples onto the silica wafer using a pipette tip.
Incubate the silica wafers in fixation solution (2.5% glutaraldehyde in 1X PBS) at 4 °C overnight.
Incubate the samples in ethanol solution with an increasing concentration (40%, 70%, 96%, 100%) for 01:00:00 each concentration.
Dry the samples at room temperature for 01:00:00 .
Use a precision etching coating system, sputtering the samples with Au-Pd alloy. If samples contain gold, sputter the samples with chromium.
The samples are ready to be visualized using SEM
creator: An.Huang
